brief_justification: Correctly identifies D with valid reasoning that reverse causality doesn't eliminate forward causality; goes one level beyond surface by noting both mechanisms can operate simultaneously but doesn't trace second-order implications or why D is strictly better than B; no explicit reflection on reasoning method or potential biases; no counterfactual checks or sanity checks applied; acknowledges multiple possible outcomes for the struggling company but lacks structured confidence analysis; implicitly references options A and C outcomes but does not systematically evaluate and reject each alternative option; the reasoning chain is mostly traceable but some intermediate steps (why B is wrong specifically) are skipped
